639.reason: [hasNextToken() returns whether there is a token after the next one, not whether current tokens are valid; this causes incorrect loop termination and potential null token access when at end of input]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [hasNextToken() returns whether there is a token after the next one, not whether current tokens are valid; this causes incorrect loop termination and potential null token access when at end of input]

393.reason: [This condition incorrectly returns false when there is exactly one token remaining to be processed (at index size-1), causing premature termination of parsing. It should be 'pos < resolvedList.getTokenList().size()' to properly indicate if there's any token left to read.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition incorrectly returns false when there is exactly one token remaining to be processed (at index size-1), causing premature termination of parsing. It should be 'pos < resolvedList.getTokenList().size()' to properly indicate if there's any token left to read.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition incorrectly returns false when pos is at the last token (index size-1), causing parsers to think there are no more tokens when there actually is one remaining token to process. It should be 'pos < resolvedList.getTokenList().size()' to properly indicate if there's a token available at the current position.]

3952.reason: [This reports the error on the wrong line. According to spec, error line should be where the last non-terminal before semicolon is located, but looking back 2 tokens and moving forward 1 may not point to the correct line of the last meaningful token (like the Exp or LVal). Should use the line number of the last parsed expression instead.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [This reports the error on the wrong line. According to spec, error line should be where the last non-terminal before semicolon is located, but looking back 2 tokens and moving forward 1 may not point to the correct line of the last meaningful token (like the Exp or LVal). Should use the line number of the last parsed expression instead.]

662.reason: [This line may cause issues because after parsing a declaration, the token pointer might be at an unexpected position, and the lookahead logic doesn't properly handle the transition between declarations and function definitions]
